role: Minister of Economic Affairs and Finance (2008–2013); managed Iran's economy through harshest sanctions period
bio:
Shamseddin Hosseini is an Iranian economist and politician who served as Minister of Economic Affairs and Finance from 2008 to 2013 under [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad). He managed Iran's economy during the period of harshest international sanctions, which severely impacted the banking sector and caused the rial to collapse.

Hosseini holds a PhD in economics from [Islamic Azad University](/orgs/islamic-azad-university) and previously taught at several Iranian universities. Before his ministerial appointment, he served as deputy minister of social welfare and subsidies and as director general for economic studies at the [Ministry of Commerce](/orgs/ministry-of-commerce).

He faced impeachment proceedings in the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) over lax banking supervision related to a major embezzlement scandal, but survived the vote after parliamentary speaker Ali Larijani intervened on the government's behalf.

sources.title: Iran's Biggest Booster: its Finance Minister

editorial_notes: Born 1967. PhD economics Islamic Azad University. Survived impeachment over 2011 bank embezzlement scandal. Minister of Economy (not just finance) — full title is Minister of Economic Affairs and Finance.
